title: chrysothamnus parryi
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi, also known as Pygmy or Pary's Chrysothamnus, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Chrysothamnus parryi is used in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and triterpenoids. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Chrysothamnus parryi is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
